seo.title: Building Manifest V3 extension development with Chrome Ex...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Manifest V3 extension development with Chrome Extension API and Manifest V3.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to building Chrome extensions with Manifest V3, secure AI integration, and cross-browser compatibility. Focuses on practical implementation steps, security patterns, and deployment workflows for developers working on productivity tools, AI-powered features, and SaaS integrations.

steps.title: Initialize project with Vite and React
steps.explanation: Create a new project using Vite with React and TypeScript template. Configure project to use Chrome Extension API by adding necessary devDependencies (e.g., @types/chrome).

code.content:
npm create vite@latest my-extension --template react-ts
cd my-extension
npm install -D @types/chrome

pitfalls: Forgetting to add @types/chrome for TS type support
Using incompatible Vite plugins for extension builds

steps.title: Configure Manifest V3 structure
steps.explanation: Create manifest.json with required fields. Replace background page with service worker, define host permissions, and set content security policy.
pitfalls: Missing required permissions for API calls

steps.title: Implement secure API key management
steps.explanation: Store API keys in chrome.storage.local instead of client code. Create a utility function to retrieve credentials securely.
pitfalls: Hardcoding API keys in client code

steps.title: Integrate AI API in service worker
steps.explanation: Create a service worker that handles API requests. Use fetch() with proper headers and error handling for AI API interactions.
pitfalls: Forgetting to handle CORS in service workers

steps.title: Add cross-browser compatibility
steps.explanation: Use WebExtension APIs instead of Chrome-specific APIs. Test in Firefox and Safari using browser-specific developer tools.

steps.title: Implement content security policy
steps.explanation: Configure content_security_policy in manifest to prevent XSS. Use strict rules for script and object loading.
pitfalls: Forgetting to update CSP when adding new assets

content.conclusion: Follow these steps to create a secure, cross-browser Chrome extension with AI integration. Validate each component through testing and ensure compliance with Chrome Web Store policies before publishing.
